title: Starbucks launches employee-generated ad program on TikTok
tldr: Starbucks will pay baristas to make branded TikTok content via a Creator Network, turning frontline employees into a paid content engine. A landmark real-world test of employee-generated content at scale.
why_fresh: first-of-its-kind paid EGC program from a major brand
angle: How brands can formalize and pay for employee-generated content without killing its authenticity.

title: Study: Google AI Overviews slash clicks by 40%
tldr: New research shows AI search summaries cut publisher clickthroughs by 40% with no rise in user satisfaction, undercutting claims that AI search sends traffic to sources. A direct threat to the referral-traffic content model.
why_fresh: fresh data quantifying AI search traffic damage
angle: What a post-clickthrough web means for content strategy and how to measure value beyond traffic.

title: The demand for news video is growing (and that's a good thing for publishers)
tldr: Reuters Digital News Report data shows genuine, sustained growth in news video consumption—unlike the misleading 'pivot to video' hype a decade ago. Signals a real shift in how audiences consume news.
why_fresh: new consumption-habit data, not a rehash of old hype
angle: Why the second video wave is different—and how to build sustainable short-form news video this time.

title: How Nation Media Group Built a Short-Video Newsroom That Competes With Entertainment
tldr: A concrete case study of an African media group restructuring its newsroom around TikTok/Shorts/Reels to reach younger audiences who never visit homepages. Practical playbook for platform-native news.
angle: What newsroom restructuring for short video teaches any content team about audience-first ops.

title: Only 6% of creator content delivers strong engagement and brand-building – report
tldr: A new report finds the vast majority of creator content underperforms on both engagement and brand-building, challenging assumptions about influencer ROI. Sharp data for anyone budgeting creator campaigns.
angle: If 94% of creator content flops, what does the winning 6% actually do differently?

title: Why 'AI Productivity Gains' Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Argues that pitching AI as 'productivity gains' fails to move CMOs, CFOs, and legal—and shows how to reframe the metric per audience to unlock budget. Practical stakeholder-communication framing.
why_fresh: fresh angle on internal AI positioning
angle: How to translate AI value into the language each executive stakeholder actually cares about.

title: Career Journeys—How Hinge's Tamika Young is bringing communications to the CMO role
tldr: A profile on a comms leader stepping into the CMO seat, signaling the growing convergence of communications and marketing at the top. Relevant to Igor's executive-positioning interests.
why_fresh: routine profile but on a live structural trend
angle: Why comms leaders are becoming CMOs—and what that says about the primacy of narrative.

title: Beehiiv's new Cloudflare partnership gives indie journalists control over AI crawlers
tldr: Beehiiv and Cloudflare team up to give independent newsletter publishers the ability to block AI scrapers—previously only available to big publishers. Democratizes AI-content defense for solo creators.
why_fresh: new tooling shift for independent publishers
angle: How independent creators can now control whether AI trains on their work—and why they should decide deliberately.

title: Teens Find Social Media Helps Friendships, but Hurts Sleep
tldr: New Pew-based data details how teens actually experience social media—positive for friendship, negative for sleep and mental health. Useful nuance on real media-consumption habits amid ban debates.
angle: Beyond the moral panic: what teen usage patterns reveal about designing content for real human needs.

title: 'Not Built to Be a Podcast Business': Inside Dear Media's Plan to Create a Lifestyle Empire
tldr: Dear Media reframes itself from podcast network to full lifestyle brand, a case study in media business-model diversification beyond audio. Instructive for anyone building an owned-media franchise.
angle: Why podcast networks are becoming lifestyle brands—and what that means for content franchising.

title: OpenAI 'clearly in the ad business now' as it reveals future plans
tldr: OpenAI is signaling a move into advertising, a potentially seismic shift for how content gets discovered and monetized inside AI assistants. Early read on the next attention marketplace.
angle: What ads inside ChatGPT would mean for content discovery, brand visibility, and AEO.

title: The Devil Wears the PESO Model®: Why Most Companies Fake Integration
tldr: Argues most brands only pretend to run an integrated paid-earned-shared-owned model and offers three signs of genuine integration versus expensive costume. A sharp diagnostic for omnichannel comms.
angle: The gap between claiming integrated comms and actually running a system—diagnostic questions to ask.

title: How to track your brand's presence in AI search
tldr: A practical guide to measuring brand mentions, citations, and share of voice inside AI answer engines—new metrics for the AEO era. Directly useful as click-based measurement erodes.
angle: Building an AI-search share-of-voice dashboard before your competitors realize they need one.

title: 3 ways you can improve your brand storytelling
tldr: A concise take on sharpening brand narratives with more human, specific storytelling. Evergreen but a quick craft refresher aligned with people-over-polish themes.
angle: The one storytelling move that separates human brand narratives from corporate boilerplate.
